_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn to choose between different options and suggest a solution.

_source.w: We learn to choose between different options and suggest a solution.
y: Making choices between alternatives builds our decision-making skills.
Being able to propose solutions makes us active problem-solvers.
Comparing options teaches us to think carefully before deciding.
eg: We choose between two ways to build something and explain our choice.
We compare two possible solutions to a problem and pick the best one.
We suggest a solution to a class problem and explain why we think it would work.
We weigh up the good and bad things about each option before choosing.
We vote on different ideas and discuss why we made our choices.
We change our mind when someone suggests a better alternative.
